items.title: Russia Said It Captured Key Ukraine Stronghold in Donetsk Region
items.summary: Russia announced the seizure of a strategic logistics hub in the Donetsk region and claimed full control of Ukraine’s Luhansk region, days after launching its deadliest attack on Kyiv since the war began.

items.title: Energy-Hungry Asia Is Already Drawing Lessons From Iran Crisis
items.summary: There is still no lasting peace deal in the Persian Gulf, but energy-hungry Asia is already drawing energy lessons from four months of war: it needs bigger buffers, a greater diversity of fossil-fuel suppliers, and a better mix of power sources overall.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: The end of ‘just in time’? Asia rejigs supply chains post-Hormuz
items.summary:
How many crises does it take to change the way the world trades? For Asia, the answer appears to be three.
First Covid closed the factories that fed the logistics networks, then Russia’s invasion of Ukraine choked off Black Sea grain exports and sent energy prices spiralling.
Now, the US-Israel war on Iran may have hammered the final nail in the coffin of “just in time” supply chains that some eco

items.title: Australia Tried to Push Back on China. China Pushed Harder.
items.summary: Relations between Beijing and Canberra have improved over the past four years, but China’s ambassador is now warning of a ‘Cold War mentality.”

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Could Israel really build settlements in Gaza?
items.summary: After a genocidal war and famine, leading Israeli politicians say they are now ready to settle Gaza.

items.title: Trump warns American identity under ‘renewed attack’ as US turns 250
items.summary:
US President Donald Trump lashed out on Friday at what he called a fresh threat against the country’s identity, taking aim at domestic “radicals and extremists” on the eve of America’s 250th birthday.
In a speech dripping with patriotism, Trump lauded American exceptionalism and praised its past leaders, speaking at Mount Rushmore with its giant carved heads of four of his legendary predecessors.
